面.wiki_summary: Wiktionary's page for trad 麵 (flour/noodles) classifies it as a phono-semantic compound: semantic 麥 (wheat) + phonetic 面; note that 面 (face) and 麵 (noodles) are distinct characters and the trad field appears to be incorrect.
面.difference: Our data classifies 面 as a pictograph (correct for 面 the face character), but the trad field points to 麵 (noodles), which Wiktionary classifies as phono_semantic_compound; the trad field should likely be 面, not 麵.

起.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with semantic 走 (walk/move) and phonetic 巳.
起.difference: Our note names the phonetic component as 己, but Wiktionary identifies it as 巳; these are visually similar but distinct characters.

看.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound of 手 (hand) over 目 (eye), representing shielding the eyes with a hand to gaze into the distance.

定.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with semantic 宀 (roof/house) and phonetic 正, meaning to settle or fix.

天.wiki_summary: Wiktionary notes the formation is debated but lists ideogrammic compound (大 + 上) as one interpretation, representing the area above a person's head.

分.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound of 八 (to separate) and 刀 (knife), representing dividing something with a knife.

还.wiki_summary: Phono-semantic compound with semantic 辵 (walk) and phonetic 睘, meaning to return or go back.

进.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound of 辵 (walk) and 隹 (short-tailed bird), representing forward movement since birds can only walk forward.

好.wiki_summary: Ideogrammic compound of 女 (woman) and 子 (child), considered auspicious for a woman to have a child, hence meaning 'good'.

小.wiki_summary: Wiktionary lists both pictogram (象形, depicting sand grains) and ideogram (指事, three dots) interpretations; pictograph is explicitly one of the options.
